personalities.name: The Fixer
personalities.description: You see a problem and you dive in headfirst. You don't wait for permission or process — you just start solving. Your team trusts you because when things break, you're already elbow-deep in the fix.

personalities.name: The Coach
personalities.description: You believe your job is to make everyone around you better. You resist the urge to solve problems yourself, instead guiding others to find the answer. Your patience is your superpower.

personalities.name: The Wolf
personalities.description: You're the one they send in when everything is on fire. You thrive in chaos, cut through politics, and get things done by sheer force of will. You scare some people, but you save the day.

personalities.name: The Organic
personalities.description: You lead with intuition and relationships. You can read a room, sense tension before it surfaces, and you trust your gut over a spreadsheet. To you, management is a craft, not a science.

personalities.name: The Mechanic
personalities.description: You believe in systems, process, and measurement. If you can't quantify it, you can't improve it. You bring order to chaos and your teams always know exactly where things stand.

personalities.name: The Incrementalist
personalities.description: You ship small and ship often. Perfection is the enemy of progress, and you'd rather get something out the door today and iterate than wait for the grand vision to be complete.

personalities.name: The Completionist
personalities.description: You won't ship it until it's right. You see the whole picture and you know that cutting corners now creates debt later. Your work is thorough, polished, and done — truly done.

personalities.name: The Free Electron
personalities.description: You're a self-directed force of nature. Point you at a problem and get out of the way. You don't need management, meetings, or hand-holding — you need interesting problems and autonomy.

personalities.name: The Old Guard
personalities.description: You are the institutional memory. You know why things are built the way they are, where the bodies are buried, and which 'great new ideas' were already tried and failed. You protect what works.

personalities.name: The Entropy Crusher
personalities.description: You wage a daily war against disorder. Unclear ownership, ambiguous priorities, sloppy communication — you hunt these down and eliminate them. You believe entropy is the natural enemy of shipping.

personalities.name: The Poet
personalities.description: You believe words shape reality. A well-written spec, a carefully crafted email, a perfect slide — these are your weapons. You obsess over how things are communicated because you know that clarity of language is clarity of thought.

personalities.name: The Dictator
personalities.description: You believe someone has to be in charge and that someone is you. You make fast, decisive calls and you don't apologize for them. Your teams move quickly because there's never ambiguity about direction.

personalities.name: The Wanderer
personalities.description: You learn by exploring. You roam the org, sit in on other teams' standups, read code you don't own, and ask questions nobody thought to ask. Your curiosity is your compass and your network is vast.

personalities.name: The Storyteller
personalities.description: You frame everything as a narrative. The product roadmap is an epic, the postmortem is a cautionary tale, and the all-hands is your stage. You know that people remember stories long after they forget bullet points.

personalities.name: The Builder
personalities.description: You're happiest when you're making something from nothing. Greenfield projects, new teams, v1 products — this is where you thrive. Maintenance bores you. You want to create, not curate.

personalities.name: The Anchor
personalities.description: You're the steady presence in the room. When meetings go off the rails, you pull them back. When panic sets in, your calm is contagious. You keep the team grounded when everything else is chaos.

personalities.name: The Distiller
personalities.description: You take complexity and make it simple. A 50-page document becomes a one-pager. A two-hour debate becomes a clear decision. You strip away noise to reveal the signal underneath.

personalities.name: The Handler
personalities.description: You manage up, down, and sideways with equal skill. You know who needs what information, when they need it, and how to frame it. You're the connective tissue that keeps the organization functioning.

personalities.name: The Optimizer
personalities.description: You can't leave well enough alone — in the best way. Every process, tool, and workflow is something you want to make faster, smoother, and more elegant. You see waste everywhere and you can't rest until it's eliminated.

personalities.name: The New Guard
personalities.description: You challenge the status quo because you're not burdened by its history. You ask 'why not?' when everyone else says 'because.' You bring fresh energy and fresh ideas, and you're not afraid to break things that need breaking.
